matches.snippet: flispering in the nightleaves flattery, dinsiduously, to Finnegan, to sin again
matches.reasoning: Direct reference to Finnegan, the eponymous figure; part of the wake ballad structure invoking the hero by name

matches.snippet: to Finnegan, to sin again and to make grim grandma grunt and grin again
matches.reasoning: Homophones 'sin/Finn' and 'again/again' + 'Finnegan' = Finn-again; the wake ballad refrain structure invoking resurrection through repetition

matches.snippet: our hugest commercial emporialist, with his sons booing home from afar and his daughters bridling up at his side. Finner!
matches.reasoning: Variant spelling of Finnegan/Finn; apostrophe-like exclamation at climax of HCE's commercial/empire-building narrative, avatar epithet

matches.snippet: The old breeding bradsted culminwillth of natures to Foyn MacHooligan. The leader, the leader!
matches.reasoning: Garbled spelling: 'Foyn' (Fionn) + 'MacHooligan' (MacCool + Irish -igan suffix); direct avatar of the Finn/HCE figure, invoked as 'the leader'

matches.snippet: wook doom for husky! And let Billey Feghin be baallad out of his humuluation.
matches.reasoning: Possible pun: 'Billey' (billow, bill, barrel—the barrel/cask in HCE's pub) + 'Feghin' (Finn sound); cryptic epithet; 'baallad out' echoes ballad form of Finnegan's Wake tradition

matches.snippet: busynext man that came on the cop with the fenian's bark that pickled his widow
matches.reasoning: Reference to the Fenians/Fianna (Finn's legendary warrior band); mythic attribute of the Finn cycle; 'fenian' carries the legendary warrior associations
